heading: Limited appointments
label: 3949 Limited appointments

children.label: a
children.text: A limited appointment in the Service, including an appointment of an individual who is an employee of an agency, may not exceed 5 years in duration and, except as provided in subsections (b) and (c), may not be extended or renewed. A limited appointment in the Service which is limited by its terms to a period of one year or less is a temporary appointment.
in accordance with ;section 3951(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: continued service is determined appropriate to remedy a matter that would be cognizable as a grievance under subchapter XI; or

children.label: B
children.text: the individual is serving in the uniformed services (as defined in ) and the limited appointment expires in the course of such service;section 4303 of title 38
as a career employee in another Federal personnel system serving in a Foreign Service position on detail from another agency;

children.label: A
children.text: a limited noncareer appointment for a period not to exceed 1 year; or

children.label: B
children.text: a limited appointment of a career candidate for the minimum time needed to resolve a grievance, claim, investigation, or complaint not otherwise provided for in this section.

Except as provided in paragraph (2) noncareer employees who have served for 5 consecutive years under a limited appointment under this section may be reappointed to a subsequent noncareer limited appointment if there is at least a 1-year break in service before such new appointment.
The Secretary may waive the 1-year break requirement under paragraph (1) in cases of special need.
